title: 9 Herbal Essential Oils For Itchy Skin
intro_desc: People with psoriasis often deal with itchy skin, which can be really uncomfortable and disrupt their daily lives. The itchy skin can be due to the inflammation and skin cell buildup that occurs in psoriasis. This can lead to scratching, which can further irritate the skin and even cause bleeding. As a result, people with psoriasis may feel embarrassed, self-conscious, and frustrated with their condition. They may avoid social situations or activities that they enjoy, or even feel isolated from others due to the constant itching and discomfort. The itchy skin can also keep them up at night, making it hard to get a good rest. This can affect their mood, energy levels, and overall well-being.  Herbal essential oils have been found to be beneficial in relieving itchy skin associated with psoriasis. These oils are extracted from plants and have natural anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. They can help calm the skin, reduce redness and irritation, and promote a sense of relaxation. When applied topically, these oils can penetrate deep into the skin, providing relief from itching and discomfort. They can also help reduce stress and anxiety, which can trigger or worsen psoriasis symptoms. By incorporating herbal essential oils into their skincare routine, people with psoriasis may find some relief from itchy skin and be able to manage their symptoms more effectively.
intro_image_alt: herbal essential oils for itchy skin

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis essential oils contains triterpenoid saponins, flavonoids, and carotenoids that help soothe and calm itchy skin. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which reduce redness and irritation, and prevent infection. The main constituents, including calendulin and triterpenoids, help to calm and protect the skin, making it an effective treatment for itchy skin caused by minor cuts, scrapes, and skin irritations. If you have itchy skin, try using Calendula officinalis essential oils by diluting a few drops with a carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, and applying it to the affected area. You can also add a few drops to a warm bath or use it as a compress. To get the best results, apply the oil or solution to clean, dry skin, and massage it in gently. When you take action and use Calendula officinalis essential oils for itchy skin, you can expect to see a reduction in redness and inflammation, and a decrease in itching and discomfort. The oil may also help to speed up the healing process, promoting healthy skin regeneration and reducing the risk of infection. As the skin begins to heal, you may notice a decrease in itching and discomfort, allowing you to focus on other activities and enjoy a more comfortable life.
remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal essential oils for itchy skin

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils contains compounds like cineole and terpinen-4-ol that make it effective in soothing itchy skin. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help reduce redness and swelling, and prevent infections that can make itching worse. When you apply Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils to itchy skin, the compounds penetrate deep into the skin, calming the itch and promoting healing. To use Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils for itchy skin, dilute a few drops of the oil with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil and apply it to the affected area. You can also add a few drops to your bath water for a relaxing soak. Be sure to do a patch test first to check for any allergic reactions. When you use Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils for itchy skin, you may start to feel relief from the itch within a few hours. The inflammation and redness should decrease, and you may be able to scratch less often. In some cases, the itch may completely go away, especially if it's caused by a skin infection or irritation. However, if the itch persists or is severe, you should consult a doctor to rule out any underlying conditions that may require medical attention.
remedies.image_alt: Melaleuca alternifolia herbal essential oils for itchy skin

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oils contains a combination of properties and constituents that make it effective in soothing itchy skin. It is rich in linalool and linalyl acetate, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. These components help to calm the skin, reduce redness, and prevent further irritation. Additionally, the oil's cooling effect can provide quick relief from itchy skin, making it an ideal remedy for conditions such as eczema, sunburn, and minor insect bites. To use Lavandula angustifolia essential oils for itchy skin, mix a few drops with a carrier oil like coconut or jojoba oil and apply it directly to the affected area. You can also add it to your bath water for a relaxing and soothing experience. For a more targeted approach, you can also mix it with a cream or lotion and apply it to the itchy area. When applying the oil, gently massage it into the skin to help it absorb and penetrate deeper. As the oil works to calm and soothe the skin, you can expect to see a reduction in itching and redness within a short period of time. The skin may also appear smoother and more even in tone. In some cases, you may also notice a decrease in the frequency and severity of itchy episodes. However, it's essential to note that Lavandula angustifolia essential oils may not completely eliminate itchy skin conditions, but it can provide significant relief and support in managing symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal essential oils for itchy skin

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis essential oils contains anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help to relieve itchy skin. The main constituents, such as aloin, aloe-emodin, and aloe-annone, have been shown to calm the skin and reduce redness. These compounds work by reducing the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that cause pain and inflammation. If you're experiencing itchy skin, try applying a few drops of Aloe barbadensis essential oils to the affected area after bathing or showering. You can also mix it with a carrier oil, like coconut or olive oil, to create a soothing moisturizer. Be sure to dilute the essential oil with a carrier oil, as it can be quite strong. Taking action with Aloe barbadensis essential oils may lead to a reduction in itching and inflammation, as well as improved skin hydration. The anti-inflammatory properties may also help to soothe sunburns, eczema, and other skin irritations. Additionally, Aloe barbadensis essential oils may help to promote wound healing and reduce scarring.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal essential oils for itchy skin

remedies.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica essential oils contains anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties that help alleviate itchy skin. The main constituents, such as isorhapontigenin and kaempferol, reduce inflammation and soothe skin irritations. When used topically, Urtica dioica essential oils can be applied directly to itchy areas, usually 1-2% dilution in a carrier oil, and gently massaged into the skin.  To use it, add a few drops of the oil to a warm bath or mix it with a carrier oil like coconut or sweet almond oil. Then, apply the mixture to the itchy area and gently rub it in. The likely outcome is that the itchiness will start to subside, and the skin will feel soothed and calm. Some people also report a decrease in redness and swelling. However, if you have sensitive skin, start with a small patch test to ensure you're not allergic to the oil. Also, if your itchy skin is caused by an underlying medical condition, consult with a healthcare professional before using Urtica dioica essential oils.
remedies.image_alt: Urtica dioica herbal essential oils for itchy skin

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum essential oils contains a compound called hyperforin, which is a powerful anti-inflammatory agent that helps to soothe itchy skin. This oil also contains flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-allergic properties that can help to reduce inflammation and irritation. When applied topically, Hypericum perforatum essential oils can help to calm itchy skin and reduce redness and swelling. If you're experiencing itchy skin, you can try using Hypericum perforatum essential oils in a few different ways. First, mix a few drops of the oil with a carrier oil like coconut or jojoba oil and apply it to the affected area. You can also add a few drops of the oil to a warm bath for a relaxing soak. Additionally, you can use a compress soaked in a solution of water and Hypericum perforatum essential oils to apply directly to the itchy area. When you use Hypericum perforatum essential oils on itchy skin, you can expect to see a reduction in itching and inflammation, as well as a decrease in redness and swelling. You may also notice that your skin feels cooler and more comfortable, and that the itching sensation is less intense. It's also possible that you'll experience a decrease in the frequency and severity of itchy skin episodes over time, as the oil helps to calm and soothe the skin.
remedies.image_alt: Hypericum perforatum herbal essential oils for itchy skin

remedies.remedy_desc: Chamomilla recutita essential oils contains anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help soothe itchy skin. This oil is rich in constituents like apigenin, bisabolol, and chamazulene, which have been shown to reduce redness and inflammation. When applied topically, Chamomilla recutita essential oils can help calm itchy skin, relieve discomfort, and promote healing. If you're experiencing itchy skin, you can try adding a few drops of Chamomilla recutita essential oils to a carrier oil like coconut or jojoba oil, and gently massage it onto the affected area. You can also add it to your bath water or use it in a compress to help soothe itchy skin. However, make sure to dilute the essential oil with a carrier oil, as it can be quite potent. By taking action and using Chamomilla recutita essential oils, you can likely experience relief from itchy skin within a few hours. The oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce redness and swelling, while its antiseptic properties can help prevent infection. As the oil works to soothe and calm the skin, you may notice a decrease in itching and discomfort, allowing you to feel more comfortable and relaxed.
remedies.image_alt: Chamomilla recutita herbal essential oils for itchy skin

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera essential oils contains soothing properties that help with itchy skin due to its high concentration of anti-inflammatory compounds like aloin, aloe-emodin, and aloe-barbaloin. These constituents have been shown to reduce redness, swelling, and irritation, providing quick relief from itchy skin. When you apply Aloe vera essential oils to itchy skin, it forms a protective barrier that locks in moisture and calms the skin, reducing the urge to scratch.  To use Aloe vera essential oils for itchy skin, mix a few drops with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil and gently massage it into the affected area. You can also add a few drops to a bath for a relaxing soak. Be sure to do a patch test before using it extensively.  When you take action, you can expect to experience a reduction in itching and discomfort, with the skin feeling smoother and more hydrated. The anti-inflammatory properties may also help reduce redness and swelling, promoting faster healing. As the skin calms down, you may notice a decrease in the frequency and intensity of itchy episodes.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe vera herbal essential oils for itchy skin

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus essential oils contains compounds like eucalyptol, which has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. These properties help reduce redness and swelling, making itchy skin feel more comfortable. Eucalyptol also has a cooling effect that can soothe itchy skin. When you apply eucalyptus globulus essential oils to itchy skin, you can take a few actions. First, mix a few drops of the oil with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil. Then, gently massage the mixture onto the itchy area. You can also add a few drops of the oil to your bath water for a relaxing soak. After taking these actions, you can expect to feel a decrease in the intensity of the itch. Your skin may also appear less red and inflamed. You may be able to sleep better at night or go about your daily activities without being distracted by itchy skin. However, if the itch persists or worsens, you should consult a healthcare professional for further advice.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal essential oils for itchy skin

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help soothe itchy skin. Certain teas, like peppermint and chamomile, have anti-inflammatory properties that can calm irritated skin. These teas can also help relax the body and mind, reducing stress that can contribute to skin issues. Some people find that drinking herbal tea regularly can keep their skin feeling calm and smooth.
faq_safe: Herbal teas can be soothing for itchy skin, but consuming them daily may not be safe for everyone. Some teas, like chamomile or calendula, are generally considered okay for regular use. However, others, like tea tree oil, should be used in moderation due to their potency. It's always best to read labels and use common sense.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can start to help with itchy skin in as little as a few hours, but it may take a few days to see noticeable results. Some people find relief from itchy skin within 24 to 48 hours after drinking herbal teas regularly. The time it takes can vary depending on the individual and the type of herbal tea.
faq_time: If you have itchy skin, drinking herbal tea in the evening might help you relax and calm your skin. This can make it easier to fall asleep and wake up with less itchiness. Some herbs, like chamomile and calendula, have anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe itchy skin.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for itchy skin. This is because some herbs can affect how the body processes the medication, changing its strength and effectiveness. For example, some herbal teas contain compounds that may slow down or speed up the liver's job of breaking down the medication.
faq_pregnant: Herbal teas can be a tempting solution for itchy skin during pregnancy. However, some herbs may not be safe for your baby. For example, peppermint and chamomile teas are usually okay, but teas like sage and licorice can cause problems. It's best to stick with teas that have been proven safe for pregnancy.
faq_breastfeeding: When breastfeeding, it's a good idea to check the ingredients of herbal teas you consume. Some herbal teas, like peppermint and chamomile, are generally safe to drink while breastfeeding. However, others like sage, parsley, and dill may affect milk supply or have other side effects, so it's best to limit or avoid them.
title_2: Itchy Skin in Psoriasis? Try These 9 Herbal Essential Oils
